players.name: Dominik Livaković
players.bio: Croatia's first-choice stopper and penalty shootout hero of Qatar 2022, Livaković saved three spot-kicks against Japan. The Zadar-born keeper returned to Dinamo Zagreb after spells abroad and remains the Vatreni's last line of defence.
players.facts: Saved 3 penalties vs Japan at Qatar 2022 — Croatia's shootout king has nerves made of Adriatic stone.

players.name: Josip Stanišić
players.bio: Born in Munich and eligible for Germany, Stančić chose Croatia — and Bayern Munich kept him on. A modern, attack-minded right-back comfortable in elite football, he brings Bundesliga pedigree to Croatia's defensive setup at just 26.
players.facts: Born in Munich, raised Vatreni — Stanišić chose Croatia over Germany, much to Bayern's indifference.

players.name: Marin Pongračić
players.bio: The Landshut-born centre-back found his feet in Serie A with Fiorentina after spells across Europe. Composed and physical, Pongračić provides aerial dominance and a cool head — qualities Croatia's back line always demands.
players.facts: Born in Bavaria, plays in Florence — Pongračić is a true European patchwork of footballing identity.

players.name: Joško Gvardiol
players.bio: One of Europe's most exciting defenders, Gvardiol is a left-footed centre-back with the engine of a midfielder and the confidence of someone twice his age. A Manchester City regular, he's Croatia's cornerstone for the next decade.
players.facts: Was named the best young defender at Qatar 2022 aged just 20 — Pep Guardiola then spent £77m. Smart lad.

players.name: Duje Ćaleta-Car
players.bio: The Šibenik-born stopper has carved out a solid career across Europe's top leagues. Ćaleta-Car's reading of the game and composure make him a reliable option, and Real Sociedad form has kept him competitive in Croatia's squad.
players.facts: Has played top-flight football in France, England and Spain — football's answer to a well-stamped passport.

players.name: Josip Šutalo
players.bio: A cultured, left-footed defender capable of playing across the backline, Šutalo established himself at Ajax after impressing with Dinamo Zagreb. Comfortable in possession, he fits Croatia's build-up style perfectly.
players.facts: Made the move from Dinamo Zagreb to Ajax — following a well-worn Croatian path to Amsterdam's famous academy club.

players.name: Nikola Moro
players.bio: A technically gifted central midfielder with an eye for goal, Moro has developed steadily at Bologna in Serie A. With just 10 caps, this World Cup could be his breakthrough on the grandest stage.
players.facts: Shares his name with an electric truck company that went bankrupt — unlike Moro's career, which is very much running.

players.name: Mateo Kovačić
players.bio: One of Europe's finest midfielders of his generation, Kovačić has won Champions Leagues, Premier Leagues and a World Cup runners-up medal. His tireless engine and silky technique make him Croatia's heartbeat with over 100 caps.
players.facts: Won the Champions League with Real Madrid and the Premier League with Manchester City — a serial winner with 113 caps.

players.name: Andrej Kramarić
players.bio: Croatia's reliable fox in the box, Kramarić has been banging in goals for Hoffenheim and the Vatreni for years. With 36 international goals in 116 caps, he's one of Croatia's greatest ever scorers and a seasoned World Cup performer.
players.facts: Has spent most of his peak years at Hoffenheim — proof you don't need a glamour club to become a national hero.

players.name: Luka Modrić
players.bio: A living legend. Modrić, the 2018 Ballon d'Or winner and FIFA Best Player, captains Croatia at an extraordinary 40 years old. Now at AC Milan, the Vatreni maestro is playing what many expect to be his final World Cup.
players.facts: At 40, Modrić is the oldest outfield player at WC2026 — still dictating games that started before some teammates were born.

players.name: Ante Budimir
players.bio: Born in Zenica in what was Yugoslavia, the powerful Budimir has become a cult hero at Osasuna. A battering-ram centre-forward who never stops running, he brings muscle and determination to Croatia's attack at 34.
players.facts: A cult hero in Pamplona — Budimir once scored 7 goals in 7 games for Osasuna, earning superstar status in Navarra.

players.name: Ivor Pandur
players.bio: The Rijeka-born goalkeeper plies his trade in the English Championship with Hull City, still awaiting his senior international debut. Uncapped but ambitious, Pandur is ready to serve as a dependable backup at WC2026.
players.facts: A Croatian international goalkeeper playing in the English Championship — Hull City's most exotic export since... ever.

players.name: Nikola Vlašić
players.bio: The Split-born attacking midfielder is technically brilliant with a powerful shot and creative instincts. Vlašić has sparkled at CSKA Moscow, West Ham and now Torino, bringing flair and goals to Croatia's midfield.
players.facts: Son of Croatian Olympic sprinter Joško Vlašić — athleticism clearly runs in the family, just in different directions.

players.name: Ivan Perišić
players.bio: The left-sided veteran has been Croatia's engine for over a decade, famous for his World Cup final performance in 2018. Now at PSV at 37, Perišić's lung-busting runs and big-game mentality remain priceless assets.
players.facts: Scored in the 2018 World Cup final — and in the 2021 Champions League final. The man loves the big occasion.

players.name: Mario Pašalić
players.bio: Born in Mainz, Pašalić has become a key figure for both Atalanta and Croatia with his energetic, box-to-box style and eye for goal. A Champions League winner with Atalanta in 2024, he's one of Croatia's most experienced midfielders.
players.facts: Won the 2024 UEFA Champions League with Atalanta — the ultimate underdog story, and Pašalić was right in the middle of it.

players.name: Martin Baturina
players.bio: The youngest creative spark in Croatia's squad, Baturina was born in Zurich and emerged through Dinamo Zagreb before moving to Como. A gifted dribbler and passer, he's tipped as the heir to Croatia's great midfield tradition.
players.facts: Born in Zurich, became a star in Zagreb — Baturina is Como's rising gem and Croatia's next great creative hope.

players.name: Petar Sučić
players.bio: The younger Sučić has rapidly become a hot prospect after breaking through at Inter Milan. Quick, technically assured and with a big future ahead, Petar brings energy and ambition to Croatia's midfield ranks at just 22.
players.facts: Playing for Inter Milan at 22 — Petar Sučić has gone straight from prospect to Serie A starter without blinking.

players.name: Kristijan Jakić
players.bio: A combative defensive midfielder from Split, Jakić has carved out a solid Bundesliga career with Augsburg. He does the dirty work others avoid — breaking up play, tracking runners and keeping Croatia's midfield tidy.
players.facts: Jakić has scored 2 international goals — remarkable for a defensive midfielder whose job description is to stop them.

players.name: Toni Fruk
players.bio: A product of the Croatian domestic scene, the Našice-born Fruk has shown creative quality at Rijeka and earned early international recognition. Still only 25, WC2026 would be a giant leap on a rapidly growing career.
players.facts: Plays his club football in Croatia's HNL — one of the few in this squad not exported to a foreign league. Local pride!

players.name: Igor Matanović
players.bio: The Hamburg-born striker made his name in the Bundesliga with Freiburg and quickly caught Croatia's eye. Young, pacey and composed in front of goal, Matanović looks set to be the Vatreni's attacking future.
players.facts: Born in Hamburg, plays for SC Freiburg — Matanović is one of four Croatia squad members born in Germany.

players.name: Luka Sučić
players.bio: Born in Linz, Austria, the elder Sučić brother has settled in at Real Sociedad with assured performances in La Liga. A midfield playmaker with elegance and positional intelligence, he's building a fine international record.
players.facts: Born in Austria, plays in Spain for Croatia — Luka Sučić is a true child of European football's borderless era.

players.name: Luka Vušković
players.bio: Arguably the most exciting teenager in this squad, Vušković is only 19 but has already turned heads with his commanding performances for Hamburg and Croatia. A defender with elite instincts and a rare composure for his age.
players.facts: Born in 2007 — Vušković is so young that Luka Modrić had already won the Ballon d'Or before he turned 12.

players.name: Dominik Kotarski
players.bio: The Zabok-born goalkeeper has been steadily building his reputation with Copenhagen, earning a handful of senior caps. A calm and technically sound keeper, Kotarski is ready to provide trustworthy cover at WC2026.
players.facts: Kept goal in Denmark's Superliga — the cold Nordic conditions apparently suit this Zabok-born stopper just fine.

players.name: Marco Pašalić
players.bio: Younger brother of Mario Pašalić, Marco has forged his own path as a right winger, landing in MLS with Orlando City. A tricky, direct winger with flair, he's one of only a handful of MLS players at WC2026.
players.facts: The Pašalić brothers are both in Croatia's squad — footballing sibling rivalry is alive and well in the Vatreni camp.

players.name: Martin Erlić
players.bio: Zadar-born Erlić has earned his caps through consistent performances in Scandinavia with Midtjylland. A reliable, no-nonsense centre-back who reads the game well, he provides depth and experience in Croatia's defensive options.
players.facts: Playing in Denmark's Superliga for Midtjylland — Erlić proves Croatia's talent pipeline reaches into Scandinavian football too.

players.name: Petar Musa
players.bio: The Zagreb-born striker made waves in Liga Portugal before moving to MLS with FC Dallas. A physical, target-man forward, Musa brings a direct presence up front and offers Croatia a different attacking option off the bench.
players.facts: Scored for Benfica in the Champions League — then swapped Lisbon for Dallas. A man of adventurous career choices.
